message.content:
Plan a fancy, visually appealing recipe book single-page website. The project directory is empty at /Users/user/Documents/Projects/recipe-book/.

Requirements:
- Fancy, modern, visually polished design
- Include figures/images for recipes (use placeholder images from picsum.photos or similar)
- Pure HTML + CSS + vanilla JS (no build tools, no frameworks — just open index.html in browser)
- Responsive layout
- Recipe cards with images in a grid
- Clicking a card opens a recipe detail modal/view with ingredients and steps
- Search/filter by category
- At least 6 sample recipes with different categories (breakfast, lunch, dinner, dessert, etc.)
- Use CSS animations/transitions for polish
- Dark/warm color palette for a premium cookbook feel

Design the file structure, the data model for recipes, the UI layout, and key CSS techniques to make it look fancy. Be specific about what goes in each file.

content.text: I'll explore the project directory to confirm its state, then design a comprehensive plan for this recipe book website.

Act 60 — Creating a Recipe Grid Layout… | by Princess Rodiel | Medium","url":"https://medium.com/@cssjhnnamae/code-recipe-grid-07c918d35544"},{"title":"How To Create Column Cards","url":"https://www.w3schools.com/howto/howto_css_column_cards.asp"},{"title":"Responsive Card Grid Layout","url":"https://codepen.io/mahmudulhrabby/pen/GGqdvr"},{"title":"Building a Responsive Card Layout with Flexbox and CSS Grid | by Aryan Vora | Medium","url":"https://medium.com/@AryanVora/building-a-responsive-card-layout-with-flexbox-and-css-grid-d3c1121cb2b9"},{"title":"Realizing common layouts using grids - CSS - MDN Web Docs","url":"https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/CSS_grid_layout/Realizing_common_layouts_using_grids"},{"title":"Build a Responsive Modal using CSS 🚀 - DEV Community","url":"https://dev.to/ananiket/build-a-responsive-modal-using-css-1h5k"},{"title":"Creating Responsive Grid Vanilla using HTML,CSS and Bootstrap - GeeksforGeeks","url":"https://www.geeksforgeeks.org/creating-responsive-grid-vanilla-using-htmlcss-and-bootstrap/"}]

I've performed a web search for "CSS grid recipe card layout responsive design vanilla JS modal". Here are the key findings:

## CSS Grid Recipe Card Layouts

A responsive recipe grid layout uses CSS Grid to create a layout that adjusts from 2 columns on mobile to 5 columns on desktop. Recipe cards are styled with a white background, rounded corners, and shadow, with a hover effect that raises the card and adds a stronger shadow for interaction.

## Responsive Design Approach

Many layouts are essentially sets of "cards" like product listings and image galleries, and a grid enables creating these listings in a way that is responsive without needing to add media queries. You can combine CSS grid and flexbox layouts to make a basic product listing layout.

## Vanilla JavaScript & Modals

For modal implementations, you can create a responsive modal popup box with a clean design using HTML as markup, CSS for styling and vanilla JavaScript for adding functionality. Grid galleries can include a native JavaScript lightbox modal.

## Code Examples

Some implementations use CSS grid to organize cards, vanilla CSS variables to control scrolling, and much less JavaScript. There are numerous free resources available on sites like CodePen and FreeFrontEnd that demonstrate these patterns with working code examples.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

I found comprehensive information about modern recipe website CSS design techniques with dark and warm palettes for 2025-2026. Here's what the latest design trends show:

## Color Palette Trends for Dark & Warm Designs (2026)

Pure black is fading out in favor of softer dark palettes built around charcoal, deep navy, and warm off-blacks, with these darker schemes feeling easier on the eyes, more premium, and better suited for long sessions and mobile use, where dark mode in 2026 is about comfort, not drama.

For warm palettes specifically, warm browns, clay reds, forest greens, and deep teals are showing up everywhere, signaling stability, reliability, and real-world grounding. This is particularly relevant for recipe websites.

## Modern CSS Design Techniques

Modern design systems rely on HSL variables, which allow you to recolor an entire layout instantly, maintain consistent UI patterns, and support light and dark themes without rebuilding from scratch. Many modern UI frameworks and CSS tools use HSL for this reason, because color becomes flexible, scalable, and simple to maintain over time.

## Key Design Principles for 2026

Designers are pairing muted neutrals with confident pops of color, such as Mocha Mousse bases with terracotta reds, deep teals, or rich greens layered intentionally. In 2026, color is less about "pretty palettes" and more about systems: colors that adapt across light/dark modes, support accessibility, and scale across brand touchpoints, making web design color trends about emotion, performance, and usability.

Accessibility is non-negotiable: contrast, states, focus indicators, and non-color cues must be baked in.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

content.text:
Now I have a solid understanding of current design trends and techniques. Let me design a comprehensive implementation plan. --- ## Recipe Book Single-Page Website -- Implementation Plan ### 1. File Structure ``` /Users/user/Documents/Projects/recipe-book/ index.html -- Single HTML page: structure, semantic markup, all content css/ variables.css -- CSS custom properties: colors, spacing, typography, shadows base.css -- Reset, body, typography, utility classes layout.css -- Header, hero, grid container, footer, responsive breakpoints components.css -- Recipe cards, modal, search bar, category pills, buttons animations.css -- Keyframes, transitions, hover effects, entrance animations js/ recipes.js -- Recipe data model (array of recipe objects) app.js -- DOM manipulation: rendering, filtering, search, modal logic favicon.svg -- Simple SVG favicon (a chef hat or utensil icon inline) ``` **Rationale for splitting CSS into 5 files:** Each file has a single responsibility and stays under ~150 lines, making it easy to navigate. They are loaded in dependency order via `` tags (variables first, then base, layout, components, animations). No build tool needed -- the browser handles 5 small CSS files without issue. **Rationale for splitting JS into 2 files:** `recipes.js` is pure data with zero logic. `app.js` is pure logic with zero data. This separation means adding or editing recipes never risks breaking application behavior. --- ### 2. Color Palette and Design Tokens The palette follows the 2026 trend of warm, grounded dark themes -- charcoal backgrounds with earthy accent colors, avoiding pure black (#000). ``` /* variables.css -- CSS Custom Properties */ /* Dark warm backgrounds */ --color-bg-primary: hsl(30, 10%, 8%); /* Near-black with warm undertone */ --color-bg-secondary: hsl(30, 8%, 12%); /* Card backgrounds */ --color-bg-elevated: hsl(30, 8%, 16%); /* Hover states, modal */ --color-bg-overlay: hsla(30, 10%, 4%, 0.85); /* Modal backdrop */ /* Text hierarchy */ --color-text-primary: hsl(35, 20%, 90%); /* Off-white, warm cream */ --color-text-secondary: hsl(30, 12%, 60%); /* Muted warm gray */ --color-text-accent: hsl(28, 70%, 58%); /* Warm copper/terracotta */ /* Accent colors */ --color-accent-primary: hsl(28, 70%, 58%); /* Copper -- primary action */ --color-accent-hover: hsl(28, 75%, 48%); /* Darker copper on hover */ --color-accent-warm: hsl(15, 60%, 50%); /* Terracotta -- category pills */ --color-accent-green: hsl(150, 30%, 40%); /* Sage green -- secondary accent */ /* Borders and surfaces */ --color-border: hsl(30, 8%, 20%); /* Subtle warm border */ --color-border-hover: hsl(28, 40%, 35%); /* Highlighted border */ /* Spacing scale (8px base) */ --space-xs: 0.25rem; /* 4px */ --space-sm: 0.5rem; /* 8px */ --space-md: 1rem; /* 16px */ --space-lg: 1.5rem; /* 24px */ --space-xl: 2rem; /* 32px */ --space-2xl: 3rem; /* 48px */ --space-3xl: 4rem; /* 64px */ /* Typography */ --font-display: 'Playfair Display', Georgia, serif; /* Headings -- elegant serif */ --font-body: 'Inter', system-ui, sans-serif; /* Body -- clean sans */ --font-size-sm: 0.875rem; --font-size-base: 1rem; --font-size-lg: 1.25rem; --font-size-xl: 1.5rem; --font-size-2xl: 2rem; --font-size-3xl: 3rem; --font-size-hero: clamp(2.5rem, 5vw, 4.5rem); /* Fluid hero size */ /* Shadows */ --shadow-card: 0 4px 20px hsla(0, 0%, 0%, 0.3); --shadow-card-hover: 0 12px 40px hsla(0, 0%, 0%, 0.5); --shadow-modal: 0 20px 60px hsla(0, 0%, 0%, 0.6); /* Border radius */ --radius-sm: 0.5rem; --radius-md: 0.75rem; --radius-lg: 1rem; --radius-xl: 1.5rem; /* Transitions */ --transition-fast: 200ms ease; --transition-base: 300ms ease; --transition-slow: 500ms ease; ``` **Google Fonts loaded in `index.html`:** Playfair Display (400, 700) and Inter (400, 500, 600). These are loaded via a single `` tag to Google Fonts CDN. Playfair Display gives the cookbook an editorial, premium feel. Inter is highly legible for body text and ingredient lists. --- ### 3. Recipe Data Model Each recipe in `recipes.js` is an object in a single exported array. ```javascript /* /js/recipes.js */ const RECIPES = [ { id: "maple-french-toast", title: "Maple Glazed French Toast", category: "breakfast", time: "25 min", servings: 4, difficulty: "Easy", description: "Thick brioche slices soaked in vanilla custard, pan-fried to golden perfection, and drizzled with pure maple glaze.", image: "https://picsum.photos/seed/french-toast/600/400", ingredients: [ "4 thick slices brioche bread", "3 large eggs", "3/4 cup whole milk", "1 tsp vanilla extract", "1/2 tsp cinnamon", "2 tbsp unsalted butter", "1/4 cup pure maple syrup", "Powdered sugar for dusting" ], steps: [ "Whisk eggs, milk, vanilla, and cinnamon in a shallow bowl until smooth.", "Heat butter in a large skillet over medium heat until foaming.", "Dip each brioche slice in the custard, letting it soak for 15 seconds per side.", "Cook 3-4 minutes per side until deeply golden and crisp.", "Warm maple syrup in a small saucepan.", "Plate the toast, drizzle with warm maple syrup, and dust with powdered sugar." ] }, { id: "thai-basil-chicken", title: "Thai Basil Chicken Stir-Fry", category: "lunch", time: "20 min", servings: 2, difficulty: "Easy", description: "A fiery, aromatic stir-fry with tender chicken, Thai holy basil, and a savory-sweet sauce served over jasmine rice.", image: "https://picsum.photos/seed/thai-basil/600/400", ingredients: [ "500g chicken thigh, diced", "4 cloves garlic, minced", "3 Thai chilies, sliced", "1 cup Thai basil leaves", "2 tbsp oyster sauce", "1 tbsp soy sauce", "1 tbsp fish sauce", "1 tsp sugar", "2 tbsp vegetable oil" ], steps: [ "Heat oil in a wok over high heat until smoking.", "Add garlic and chilies, stir-fry for 30 seconds until fragrant.", "Add chicken and spread in a single layer. Sear without stirring for 2 minutes.", "Toss and continue cooking until chicken is no longer pink, about 3 minutes.", "Add oyster sauce, soy sauce, fish sauce, and sugar. Stir to coat evenly.", "Kill the heat, toss in basil leaves, and fold until just wilted.", "Serve immediately over steamed jasmine rice." ] }, { id: "herb-crusted-salmon", title: "Herb-Crusted Salmon with Lemon Butter", category: "dinner", time: "30 min", servings: 2, difficulty: "Medium", description: "Oven-roasted Atlantic salmon with a golden Dijon-herb crust and silky lemon butter sauce.", image: "https://picsum.photos/seed/salmon/600/400", ingredients: [ "2 salmon fillets (6 oz each)", "2 tbsp Dijon mustard", "1/2 cup panko breadcrumbs", "2 tbsp fresh parsley, chopped", "1 tbsp fresh dill, chopped", "3 tbsp unsalted butter", "Juice of 1 lemon", "Salt and pepper to taste" ], steps: [ "Preheat oven to 400F (200C). Line a baking sheet with parchment paper.", "Season salmon with salt and pepper, then spread Dijon mustard on top.", "Mix panko, parsley, and dill. Press the herb mixture firmly onto the mustard layer.", "Bake for 14-16 minutes until the crust is golden and salmon is just opaque.", "While salmon bakes, melt butter in a small pan, add lemon juice, and whisk until emulsified.", "Plate the salmon and spoon the lemon butter sauce around it." ] }, { id: "tiramisu", title: "Classic Italian Tiramisu", category: "dessert", time: "40 min + 4h chill", servings: 8, difficulty: "Medium", description: "Layers of espresso-soaked ladyfingers and velvety mascarpone cream, dusted with cocoa. No baking required.", image: "https://picsum.photos/seed/tiramisu/600/400", ingredients: [ "6 egg yolks", "3/4 cup granulated sugar", "500g mascarpone cheese", "2 cups heavy cream", "2 cups strong espresso, cooled", "3 tbsp coffee liqueur (optional)", "400g ladyfinger cookies", "Unsweetened cocoa powder" ], steps: [ "Whisk egg yolks and sugar until thick, pale, and ribbon-like, about 4 minutes.", "Add mascarpone and fold gently until completely smooth.", "In a separate bowl, whip heavy cream to stiff peaks.", "Fold whipped cream into the mascarpone mixture in three additions.", "Combine espresso and coffee liqueur in a shallow dish.", "Quickly dip each ladyfinger in espresso (do not soak) and layer in a 9x13 dish.", "Spread half the mascarpone cream over the ladyfingers. Repeat layers.", "Cover and refrigerate for at least 4 hours, preferably overnight.", "Dust generously with cocoa powder before serving." ] }, { id: "shakshuka", title: "Shakshuka with Feta and Herbs", category: "breakfast", time: "35 min", servings: 4, difficulty: "Easy", description: "Eggs gently poached in a smoky, spiced tomato sauce with crumbled feta, served with crusty bread.", image: "https://picsum.photos/seed/shakshuka/600/400", ingredients: [ "6 large eggs", "1 can (28 oz) crushed tomatoes", "1 large onion, diced", "1 red bell pepper, diced", "4 cloves garlic, minced", "2 tsp cumin", "1 tsp smoked paprika", "1/2 tsp red pepper flakes", "100g feta cheese, crumbled", "Fresh cilantro for garnish", "2 tbsp olive oil" ], steps: [ "Heat olive oil in a large oven-safe skillet over medium heat.", "Saute onion and bell pepper until softened, about 5 minutes.", "Add garlic, cumin, paprika, and red pepper flakes. Cook 1 minute until fragrant.", "Pour in crushed tomatoes, season with salt and pepper, and simmer for 10 minutes.", "Make 6 wells in the sauce and crack an egg into each.", "Cover and cook on low for 6-8 minutes until egg whites are set but yolks are still runny.", "Scatter feta and cilantro over the top. Serve straight from the skillet with crusty bread." ] }, { id: "beef-bourguignon", title: "Slow-Braised Beef Bourguignon", category: "dinner", time: "3 hours", servings: 6, difficulty: "Hard", description: "A rich French stew of tender beef, red wine, pearl onions, mushrooms, and bacon -- the ultimate comfort dish.", image: "https://picsum.photos/seed/bourguignon/600/400", ingredients: [ "1 kg beef chuck, cut into 2-inch cubes", "200g thick-cut bacon, diced", "1 bottle (750ml) dry red wine", "2 cups beef stock", "200g pearl onions, peeled", "250g cremini mushrooms, quartered", "3 carrots, cut into chunks", "3 tbsp tomato paste", "4 cloves garlic, minced", "2 tbsp flour", "Fresh thyme and bay leaves", "2 tbsp olive oil" ], steps: [ "Pat beef dry and season generously with salt and pepper.", "Sear beef in batches in a hot Dutch oven with olive oil until deeply browned on all sides. Set aside.", "In the same pot, render the bacon until crisp. Add pearl onions and carrots, cook 5 minutes.", "Stir in garlic and tomato paste, cook 2 minutes.", "Sprinkle flour over the vegetables and stir for 1 minute.", "Deglaze with the entire bottle of red wine, scraping up browned bits.", "Return the beef, add stock, thyme, and bay leaves. Bring to a simmer.", "Cover and braise in a 325F (160C) oven for 2 to 2.5 hours until beef is fork-tender.", "In the last 30 minutes, sautee mushrooms separately and add to the stew.", "Adjust seasoning and serve over buttered egg noodles or mashed potatoes." ] }, { id: "mango-sticky-rice", title: "Thai Mango Sticky Rice", category: "dessert", time: "45 min + soaking", servings: 4, difficulty: "Medium", description: "Sweet coconut-infused glutinous rice paired with ripe mango slices and a drizzle of rich coconut cream.", image: "https://picsum.photos/seed/mango-rice/600/400", ingredients: [ "2 cups glutinous (sticky) rice", "1 can (400ml) coconut milk", "1/2 cup sugar", "1/2 tsp salt", "2 ripe mangoes", "2 tbsp coconut cream", "1 tbsp toasted sesame seeds" ], steps: [ "Soak sticky rice in water for at least 4 hours or overnight.", "Drain and steam the rice in a cheesecloth-lined steamer for 20-25 minutes until tender.", "While rice steams, warm coconut milk with sugar and salt until dissolved. Do not boil.", "Transfer hot rice to a bowl and pour 3/4 of the coconut sauce over it. Fold gently.", "Cover and let the rice absorb the sauce for 15 minutes.", "Peel and slice mangoes into elegant fans.", "Mound the sticky rice on a plate, arrange mango alongside, drizzle with remaining coconut cream, and sprinkle with toasted sesame seeds." ] }, { id: "mediterranean-bowl", title: "Mediterranean Grain Bowl", category: "lunch", time: "30 min", servings: 2, difficulty: "Easy", description: "A vibrant bowl of herbed farro, roasted chickpeas, cucumber, tomatoes, olives, and creamy lemon-tahini dressing.", image: "https://picsum.photos/seed/grain-bowl/600/400", ingredients: [ "1 cup farro", "1 can chickpeas, drained", "1 cucumber, diced", "1 cup cherry tomatoes, halved", "1/2 cup kalamata olives", "100g feta cheese, cubed", "3 tbsp tahini", "Juice of 1 lemon", "2 tbsp olive oil", "1 tsp smoked paprika", "Fresh parsley and mint" ], steps: [ "Cook farro according to package directions. Drain and let cool slightly.", "Toss chickpeas with olive oil, smoked paprika, salt, and pepper. Roast at 425F for 20 minutes until crispy.", "Whisk tahini, lemon juice, 2 tbsp water, and a pinch of salt until smooth and pourable.", "Divide farro between bowls. Arrange cucumber, tomatoes, olives, feta, and crispy chickpeas on top.", "Drizzle generously with lemon-tahini dressing.", "Finish with torn fresh parsley and mint leaves." ] } ]; ``` That gives us **8 recipes** across 4 categories: breakfast (2), lunch (2), dinner (2), dessert (2). Each recipe has a unique `seed` value for picsum.photos so the placeholder image is deterministic and consistent across page loads. --- ### 4. HTML Structure (`index.html`) The HTML is semantic and minimal. All visual work is done by CSS. Structure: ``` The Recipe Book

The Recipe Book

Handpicked recipes for every occasion

No recipes found. Try a different search.

Ingredients

    Preparation

      © 2026 The Recipe Book

      ``` Key HTML decisions: - `aria-live="polite"` on the grid so screen readers announce when filtered results change. - `role="dialog"` and `aria-modal="true"` on the modal for accessibility. - The modal exists in HTML once and is populated dynamically (no cloning, no innerHTML template strings with complex HTML). - SVG icons are inline (search icon, close button) to avoid external asset dependencies. --- ### 5. CSS Architecture -- File by File #### `css/variables.css` Contains only `:root { ... }` with all custom properties listed in section 2 above. No selectors, no rules -- just tokens. #### `css/base.css` ```css /* Box-sizing reset */ *, *::before, *::after { box-sizing: border-box; } /* Remove default margins */ body, h1, h2, h3, p, ul, ol { margin: 0; } ul, ol { padding: 0; list-style: none; } /* Base body */ body { font-family: var(--font-body); font-size: var(--font-size-base); color: var(--color-text-primary); background-color: var(--color-bg-primary); line-height: 1.6; -webkit-font-smoothing: antialiased; overflow-x: hidden; } /* Headings use display font */ h1, h2, h3 { font-family: var(--font-display); } /* Smooth scrolling */ html { scroll-behavior: smooth; } /* Utility: visually-hidden for a11y */ .visually-hidden { /* sr-only pattern */ } ``` #### `css/layout.css` **Header/Hero:** - Full-width with a subtle radial gradient background (dark brown center fading to the body color). - `text-align: center`, generous vertical padding (`var(--space-3xl)`). - The title uses `font-size: var(--font-size-hero)` with `clamp()` for fluid scaling. - A decorative horizontal rule below the tagline: a thin `
      ` styled as a centered 80px line in the accent copper color. **Controls section:** - `max-width: 900px; margin: 0 auto;` to center it. - Flexbox row on desktop (search left, pills right), column on mobile. - `position: sticky; top: 0; z-index: 10;` with the background color and a subtle `backdrop-filter: blur(10px)` so it stays visible while scrolling. **Recipe Grid:** ```css .recipe-grid { display: grid; grid-template-columns: repeat(auto-fill, minmax(320px, 1fr)); gap: var(--space-xl); max-width: 1400px; margin: 0 auto; padding: var(--space-xl) var(--space-lg); } ``` `auto-fill` with `minmax(320px, 1fr)` gives a naturally responsive grid without media queries. On a 1400px screen, you get 4 columns. On tablet, 2. On phone, 1. The `320px` minimum ensures cards never get uncomfortably narrow. **Responsive breakpoints** (only a few, since the grid is intrinsically responsive): ```css @media (max-width: 768px) { .controls { flex-direction: column; gap: var(--space-md); } .site-header { padding: var(--space-2xl) var(--space-md); } } @media (max-width: 480px) { .recipe-grid { grid-template-columns: 1fr; padding: var(--space-md); } .modal { margin: 0; border-radius: 0; height: 100vh; } } ``` #### `css/components.css` **Recipe Card:** ```css .recipe-card { background: var(--color-bg-secondary); border-radius: var(--radius-lg); overflow: hidden; cursor: pointer; border: 1px solid var(--color-border); box-shadow: var(--shadow-card); transition: transform var(--transition-base), box-shadow var(--transition-base), border-color var(--transition-base); display: flex; flex-direction: column; } .recipe-card:hover { transform: translateY(-6px); box-shadow: var(--shadow-card-hover); border-color: var(--color-border-hover); } ``` Card internals: - `.card-image-wrapper` -- `aspect-ratio: 3/2; overflow: hidden;` The image inside has `width: 100%; height: 100%; object-fit: cover;` and on hover scales up slightly (`transform: scale(1.05)`) with a transition for a zoom effect. - `.card-body` -- padding with the title, description (clamped to 2 lines via `-webkit-line-clamp`), and a footer row. - `.card-footer` -- Flexbox row showing category pill, cook time, and difficulty as small badges. - `.card-category` -- A small pill/badge with background color derived from category (using a data-attribute and CSS). **Category color mapping via data attributes:** ```css .category-pill[data-category="breakfast"] { background: hsl(35, 60%, 25%); color: hsl(35, 60%, 75%); } .category-pill[data-category="lunch"] { background: hsl(150, 30%, 20%); color: hsl(150, 40%, 65%); } .category-pill[data-category="dinner"] { background: hsl(0, 40%, 25%); color: hsl(0, 50%, 70%); } .category-pill[data-category="dessert"] { background: hsl(280, 30%, 25%); color: hsl(280, 40%, 70%); } ``` **Search Input:** ```css .search-wrapper { position: relative; max-width: 400px; width: 100%; } #search-input { width: 100%; padding: var(--space-sm) var(--space-md) var(--space-sm) var(--space-2xl); background: var(--color-bg-secondary); border: 1px solid var(--color-border); border-radius: var(--radius-xl); color: var(--color-text-primary); font-size: var(--font-size-base); transition: border-color var(--transition-fast), box-shadow var(--transition-fast); } #search-input:focus { outline: none; border-color: var(--color-accent-primary); box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px hsla(28, 70%, 58%, 0.2); } ``` **Filter Pills (category buttons):** ```css .filter-pill { padding: var(--space-xs) var(--space-md); border-radius: var(--radius-xl); border: 1px solid var(--color-border); background: transparent; color: var(--color-text-secondary); cursor: pointer; font-family: var(--font-body); font-size: var(--font-size-sm); text-transform: capitalize; transition: all var(--transition-fast); } .filter-pill:hover { border-color: var(--color-accent-primary); color: var(--color-text-primary); } .filter-pill.active { background: var(--color-accent-primary); border-color: var(--color-accent-primary); color: var(--color-bg-primary); font-weight: 600; } ``` **Modal:** ```css .modal-overlay { position: fixed; inset: 0; background: var(--color-bg-overlay); display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; z-index: 100; padding: var(--space-xl); opacity: 0; visibility: hidden; transition: opacity var(--transition-base), visibility var(--transition-base); } .modal-overlay.is-active { opacity: 1; visibility: visible; } .modal { background: var(--color-bg-secondary); border-radius: var(--radius-xl); max-width: 800px; width: 100%; max-height: 90vh; overflow-y: auto; position: relative; transform: translateY(30px) scale(0.95); transition: transform var(--transition-base); box-shadow: var(--shadow-modal); } .modal-overlay.is-active .modal { transform: translateY(0) scale(1); } ``` The modal image is full-width at the top with `aspect-ratio: 16/9; object-fit: cover;`. A subtle gradient overlay sits on top of the image (using `::after` with a `linear-gradient` from transparent to `var(--color-bg-secondary)`) to blend the image into the content below. The ingredient list uses custom bullet points (small copper-colored circles via `::before` pseudo-elements). The step list uses numbered steps with the number styled as a large copper circle. **Modal close button:** ```css .modal-close { position: absolute; top: var(--space-md); right: var(--space-md); width: 40px; height: 40px; border-radius: 50%; background: hsla(0, 0%, 0%, 0.5); backdrop-filter: blur(10px); border: none; color: white; font-size: 1.5rem; cursor: pointer; z-index: 10; transition: background var(--transition-fast); } ``` #### `css/animations.css` **Card entrance animation (staggered):** ```css @keyframes fadeInUp { from { opacity: 0; transform: translateY(24px); } to { opacity: 1; transform: translateY(0); } } .recipe-card { animation: fadeInUp var(--transition-slow) ease both; } /* Stagger via JS-applied custom property */ .recipe-card { animation-delay: calc(var(--card-index) * 80ms); } ``` The JS sets `style="--card-index: 0"`, `--card-index: 1`, etc. on each card. This creates a cascade waterfall effect when cards first render or when the filter changes. **Image hover zoom:** ```css .card-image-wrapper img { transition: transform var(--transition-slow); } .recipe-card:hover .card-image-wrapper img { transform: scale(1.08); } ``` **Filter pill transition (color fills smoothly).** **Search input glow on focus (box-shadow transition).** **Modal overlay fade + modal slide-up + scale (described in components above).** **Skeleton/shimmer effect while images load:** ```css @keyframes shimmer { from { background-position: -400px 0; } to { background-position: 400px 0; } } .card-image-wrapper { background: linear-gradient( 90deg, var(--color-bg-secondary) 0%, var(--color-bg-elevated) 50%, var(--color-bg-secondary) 100% ); background-size: 800px 100%; animation: shimmer 1.5s infinite linear; } ``` Once the `` loads, it covers the shimmer. No JS needed for this -- it is pure CSS. --- ### 6. JavaScript Architecture (`app.js`) The JS file is organized as an IIFE (Immediately Invoked Function Expression) to avoid polluting the global scope. Only `RECIPES` from `recipes.js` is in the global scope. ```javascript (function() { "use strict"; // ── Constants ── const DEBOUNCE_DELAY_MS = 250; const ALL_CATEGORY = "all"; // ── DOM References ── const gridEl = document.getElementById("recipe-grid"); const searchInput = document.getElementById("search-input"); const pillsContainer = document.querySelector(".category-pills"); const modalOverlay = document.getElementById("modal-overlay"); const emptyState = document.getElementById("empty-state"); // ... other modal elements // ── State ── let activeCategory = ALL_CATEGORY; let searchQuery = ""; // ── Initialization ── function initialize() { renderCategoryPills(); renderRecipeCards(RECIPES); attachEventListeners(); } // ── Rendering ── function renderCategoryPills() { ... } /* Extracts unique categories from RECIPES, creates "All" pill plus one per category, appends to pillsContainer */ function renderRecipeCards(recipes) { ... } /* Clears gridEl, creates a card element per recipe, sets --card-index CSS variable for stagger, shows/hides empty state */ function createRecipeCard(recipe, index) { ... } /* Returns a
      element with: .card-image-wrapper > img .card-body > h2.card-title + p.card-description .card-footer > category pill + time + difficulty Sets data-recipe-id attribute Returns the element (not innerHTML string) */ // ── Filtering ── function filterRecipes() { const query = searchQuery.toLowerCase().trim(); const filtered = RECIPES.filter(recipe => { const matchesCategory = activeCategory === ALL_CATEGORY || recipe.category === activeCategory; const matchesSearch = query === "" || recipe.title.toLowerCase().includes(query) || recipe.description.toLowerCase().includes(query) || recipe.ingredients.some(ing => ing.toLowerCase().includes(query)); return matchesCategory && matchesSearch; }); renderRecipeCards(filtered); } // ── Modal ── function openModal(recipeId) { const recipe = RECIPES.find(r => r.id === recipeId); if (!recipe) return; // Populate modal elements // Add .is-active to overlay // Trap focus inside modal // Prevent body scroll (body.style.overflow = "hidden") } function closeModal() { // Remove .is-active // Restore body scroll // Return focus to the card that triggered the modal } // ── Event Listeners ── function attachEventListeners() { // Search input: debounced input event // Category pills: click delegation on pillsContainer // Recipe grid: click delegation on gridEl (find closest .recipe-card) // Modal close: click on close button, click on overlay (outside modal), Escape key } // ── Utilities ── function debounce(fn, delay) { let timer; return function(...args) { clearTimeout(timer); timer = setTimeout(() => fn.apply(this, args), delay); }; } // ── Start ── document.addEventListener("DOMContentLoaded", initialize); })(); ``` Key JS decisions: - **Event delegation** on the grid (one listener, not one per card) for performance and to handle dynamic re-renders cleanly. - **Debounced search** (250ms) so filtering does not fire on every keystroke. - **DOM element creation** via `document.createElement` rather than innerHTML strings -- safer, no XSS risk, and easier to attach data attributes. - **Focus trapping in modal** for accessibility: Tab and Shift+Tab cycle within the modal. Escape closes it. The previously focused card receives focus when the modal closes. - **No state management library** -- two variables (`activeCategory`, `searchQuery`) and a single `filterRecipes()` function is all that is needed. --- ### 7. Specific Visual Techniques for "Fancy" Polish 1. **Gradient text on the hero title:** ```css .site-title { background: linear-gradient(135deg, var(--color-text-primary), var(--color-accent-primary)); -webkit-background-clip: text; -webkit-text-fill-color: transparent; background-clip: text; } ``` 2. **Decorative accent line under the tagline:** A short horizontal bar (80px wide, 2px tall) in the copper accent color, centered, with a subtle glow (`box-shadow: 0 0 12px var(--color-accent-primary)`). 3. **Card image gradient overlay:** A `::after` pseudo-element on `.card-image-wrapper` creates a gradient from transparent at top to the card background at bottom, so the image bleeds elegantly into the text area rather than having a hard edge. 4. **Subtle texture on the body:** A CSS-only noise texture using a tiny repeating `radial-gradient` pattern at very low opacity, giving the dark background a slight paper/grain feel rather than flat digital color. 5. **Custom scrollbar styling:** ```css ::-webkit-scrollbar { width: 8px; } ::-webkit-scrollbar-track { background: var(--color-bg-primary); } ::-webkit-scrollbar-thumb { background: var(--color-border); border-radius: 4px; } ``` 6. **Numbered recipe steps in the modal** styled as large circled numbers (copper background, display font) to the left of each step text, creating a professional cookbook layout. 7. **Card metadata badges** (time, difficulty) use a subtle glass-morphism effect: `background: hsla(30, 8%, 20%, 0.6); backdrop-filter: blur(4px);`. --- ### 8. favicon.svg A simple inline SVG of a chef's hat or a fork-and-knife icon, using the copper accent color. Approximately 10 lines of SVG markup. This avoids needing any external image files. --- ### 9. Implementation Sequence | Step | File(s) | What to do | |------|---------|------------| | 1 | `css/` directory | Create the directory | | 2 | `js/` directory | Create the directory | | 3 | `css/variables.css` | Define all custom properties (colors, spacing, typography, shadows, radii, transitions) | | 4 | `css/base.css` | Write reset, body styles, typography defaults, utility classes | | 5 | `css/layout.css` | Header, controls sticky bar, grid, footer, responsive breakpoints | | 6 | `css/components.css` | Cards, search input, filter pills, modal, badges, ingredient/step lists | | 7 | `css/animations.css` | Keyframes (fadeInUp, shimmer), hover transitions, modal transitions | | 8 | `js/recipes.js` | Define the RECIPES array with all 8 recipe objects | | 9 | `js/app.js` | Initialize, render, filter, modal, event listeners, debounce utility | | 10 | `index.html` | Assemble the HTML structure, link all CSS/JS, add Google Fonts, inline SVG icons | | 11 | `favicon.svg` | Create the SVG favicon | | 12 | Test | Open in browser, verify responsive behavior, test all interactions | The CSS files are built first because they define the design system. The JS data file comes next because it is standalone. The JS app file comes after the HTML exists (since it references DOM elements). The HTML is last because it ties everything together with the correct file paths. --- ### 10. Potential Challenges and Mitigations | Challenge | Mitigation | |-----------|------------| | picsum.photos images may load slowly | The shimmer animation in `.card-image-wrapper` provides visual feedback while loading. Images use `loading="lazy"` so only visible cards load initially. | | Modal scroll on mobile (address bar, viewport issues) | Use `dvh` (dynamic viewport height) where supported, with `vh` fallback. On small screens, modal goes full-screen (border-radius: 0, height: 100dvh). | | Search performance with many recipes | Debounce at 250ms. For 8 recipes this is not an issue, but the pattern scales to hundreds. | | Accessibility | Focus trapping in modal, `aria-live` on grid, `role="dialog"` on modal, keyboard navigation for pills, visible focus outlines (copper glow ring). | | Font loading flash (FOUT) | `font-display: swap` in the Google Fonts URL. The fallback fonts (Georgia, system-ui) are visually similar enough that the swap is not jarring. | --- ### Critical Files for Implementation - `/Users/user/Documents/Projects/recipe-book/index.html` - The single HTML page that assembles all components, links CSS/JS, and defines the semantic structure including the modal skeleton - `/Users/user/Documents/Projects/recipe-book/css/variables.css` - Design token foundation that every other CSS file depends on; must be created first - `/Users/user/Documents/Projects/recipe-book/css/components.css` - The largest and most visually impactful CSS file containing card, modal, search, and pill styling - `/Users/user/Documents/Projects/recipe-book/js/recipes.js` - The data layer holding all 8 recipe objects with their full content and image URLs - `/Users/user/Documents/Projects/recipe-book/js/app.js` - All application logic: rendering, filtering, search, modal open/close, event delegation, and accessibility
